narrative.headline: 2020 Mazda Mazda3: forward collision avoidance leads the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 31 complaints with NHTSA. Forward collision avoidance is by far the most reported area at 48%, well ahead of steering, the electrical system, and the brakes, which each draw 10%. No crashes, fires, injuries, or deaths are reported in any of these areas. On a test drive, pay attention to how the automatic braking and driver-assist systems behave.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 15 reports (48%), nearly half of all filings. No crashes or injuries reported. Owners describe false braking alerts; watch how the system reacts on a test drive.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 3 reports (10%), with no crashes or injuries. Some owners describe the car taking over the steering during lane changes.

issues.blurb: The smallest area at 2 reports (6%), with no crashes or injuries. Owners note a loud metallic pop at low speed.
